name: Putnam County Housing Corporation
description_short: Non-profit housing organization providing free foreclosure prevention counseling, housing assistance, and home improvement programs to Putnam County residents since 1982.
description_long: Putnam County Housing Corporation (PCHC) was incorporated in 1982 as a non-profit Rural Preservation Company (RPC) serving the housing needs of Putnam County residents. Over 40 years of operation, the organization has established itself as a community-focused housing provider with a demonstrated track record of tangible impact. PCHC offers a comprehensive suite of housing-related services including free foreclosure prevention assistance, housing counseling, reverse mortgage guidance, home improvement financing, and senior housing development. The organization also administers the Housing Choice Voucher Program and provides fair housing education and advocacy. Their service model emphasizes accessibility, particularly for vulnerable populations including seniors and low-income homeowners. PCHC distinguishes itself through its non-profit structure, rural focus, and direct investment in community housing stock—having constructed 120 units of senior housing and 12 units of transitional/permanent housing while facilitating nearly $4 million in home improvements. The organization operates within established networks, partnering with state and federal housing authorities including HUD and New York State Housing and Community Renewal. Client testimonials reflect consistent satisfaction with their knowledgeable staff and results-driven approach. As a non-profit RPC, PCHC provides genuine free services rather than profit-driven alternatives, making it a legitimate resource for Putnam County residents facing housing instability or seeking affordable housing solutions. The primary limitation is geographic scope—services are restricted to Putnam County residents only.
pros: Free foreclosure prevention counseling and assistance—no fees for at-risk homeowners
Free housing counseling services available to county residents
Demonstrated 40-year track record with $4M+ in documented home improvements completed
Non-profit structure ensures mission-driven service without profit incentives
Constructed 120 senior housing units and 12 transitional/permanent housing units
Five-star client reviews citing knowledgeable staff and results-driven approach
Partnerships with HUD and New York State housing authorities provide institutional credibility
cons: Services limited to Putnam County residents only—geographic restrictions apply
Website lacks specific details on program eligibility requirements and application processes
No information provided on average processing times or service capacity/wait lists
Limited details on the scope and terms of reverse mortgage guidance services
No clear timeline or contact process published for foreclosure prevention assistance
best_for: Putnam County homeowners facing foreclosure seeking free prevention counseling
Senior residents in Putnam County exploring affordable housing or reverse mortgage options
Low-to-moderate income homeowners seeking assistance with home improvements or repairs
diagnosis: PCHC is best for Putnam County homeowners and seniors seeking genuinely free, non-profit housing assistance, particularly those facing foreclosure or needing home repairs. The critical caveat is strict geographic limitation—this organization serves only Putnam County residents, so out-of-area applicants must seek alternative local resources.
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
